prompts.scenario_header:
You are an expert university registrar choosing the best final-exam schedule.
Use these definitions (lower is better unless stated otherwise):
• conflicts: 2 exams at the same time (requires makeup)
• quints: 5 in a row (requires makeup)
• quads: 4 in a row (requires makeup)
• four in five slots: 4 within 5 (requires makeup)
• triple in 24h (no gaps): 3 within 24h, consecutive (requires makeup)
• triple in same day (no gaps): 3 in one day, consecutive (requires makeup)
• three in four slots: 3 within 4 slots
• evening/morning b2b: evening into next morning
• other b2b: any other back-to-back pair
• two in three slots: 2 within 3 slots
• avg_max: average slot of each student's last exam (lower is earlier finish)

prompt_vars.utility_priorities: priorities for student welfare and administrative efficiency
prompt_vars.analysis_questions:
- Does this schedule seem reasonable for student welfare and administrative efficiency?
- Are any metrics concerning given their severity levels?
- Should any penalties be increased or decreased?

REGISTRAR.prompt: *Objective:* To generate an optimal final examination schedule that minimizes student stress and administrative burden by optimizing a specific set of metrics according to a tiered priority system.  *Context:* There are three exam slots available per day. Solutions must have a conflicts value of 0 or 1.  *Tier 1:* Minimize Mandatory Reschedules The highest priority is to minimize the metrics associated with intense exam clusters that require a mandatory reschedule. Please minimize the following metrics in the order listed: quints (five exams in 24 hours) quads (four exams in 24 hours) four in five slots triple in 24h (no gaps) triple in same day (no gaps)  *Tier 2:* Reduce Student Exam Fatigue Once Tier 1 objectives have been met, the primary tie-breaker is to minimize the total number of back-to-back exams. This objective is achieved by minimizing the sum of the following two metrics: evening/morning b2b other b2b  *Tier 3:* Enhance Overall Schedule Quality As a final set of tie-breakers, prioritize schedules that improve the general student experience by optimizing these metrics in order of preference: Lower the avg_max (the average slot of a student's final exam). Minimize two in three slots. Minimize three in four slots.

Policy guidance: *Objective:* To generate an optimal final examination schedule that minimizes student stress and administrative burden by optimizing a specific set of metrics according to a tiered priority system.  *Context:* There are three exam slots available per day. Solutions must have a conflicts value of 0 or 1.  *Tier 1:* Minimize Mandatory Reschedules The highest priority is to minimize the metrics associated with intense exam clusters that require a mandatory reschedule. Please minimize the following metrics in the order listed: quints (five exams in 24 hours) quads (four exams in 24 hours) four in five slots triple in 24h (no gaps) triple in same day (no gaps)  *Tier 2:* Reduce Student Exam Fatigue Once Tier 1 objectives have been met, the primary tie-breaker is to minimize the total number of back-to-back exams. This objective is achieved by minimizing the sum of the following two metrics: evening/morning b2b other b2b  *Tier 3:* Enhance Overall Schedule Quality As a final set of tie-breakers, prioritize schedules that improve the general student experience by optimizing these metrics in order of preference: Lower the avg_max (the average slot of a student's final exam). Use these definitions (lower is better unless stated otherwise):
• conflicts: 2 exams at the same time (requires makeup)
• quints: 5 in a row (requires makeup)
• quads: 4 in a row (requires makeup)
• four in five slots: 4 within 5 (requires makeup)
• triple in 24h (no gaps): 3 within 24h, consecutive (requires makeup)
• triple in same day (no gaps): 3 in one day, consecutive (requires makeup)
• three in four slots: 3 within 4 slots
• evening/morning b2b: evening into next morning
• other b2b: any other back-to-back pair
• two in three slots: 2 within 3 slots
• avg_max: average slot of each student's last exam (lower is earlier finish)

You are an expert university registrar choosing the best final-exam schedule.
Use these definitions (lower is better unless stated otherwise):
• conflicts: 2 exams at the same time (requires makeup)
• quints: 5 in a row (requires makeup)
• quads: 4 in a row (requires makeup)
• four in five slots: 4 within 5 (requires makeup)
• triple in 24h (no gaps): 3 within 24h, consecutive (requires makeup)
• triple in same day (no gaps): 3 in one day, consecutive (requires makeup)
• three in four slots: 3 within 4 slots
• evening/morning b2b: evening into next morning
• other b2b: any other back-to-back pair
• two in three slots: 2 within 3 slots
• avg_max: average slot of each student's last exam (lower is earlier finish)


Your task: Identify the SINGLE BEST schedule from the options below.

Policy guidance: *Objective:* To generate an optimal final examination schedule that minimizes student stress and administrative burden by optimizing a specific set of metrics according to a tiered priority system.  *Context:* There are three exam slots available per day. Solutions must have a conflicts value of 0 or 1.  *Tier 1:* Minimize Mandatory Reschedules The highest priority is to minimize the metrics associated with intense exam clusters that require a mandatory reschedule. Please minimize the following metrics in the order listed: quints (five exams in 24 hours) quads (four exams in 24 hours) four in five slots triple in 24h (no gaps) triple in same day (no gaps)  *Tier 2:* Reduce Student Exam Fatigue Once Tier 1 objectives have been met, the primary tie-breaker is to minimize the total number of back-to-back exams. This objective is achieved by minimizing the sum of the following two metrics: evening/morning b2b other b2b  *Tier 3:* Enhance Overall Schedule Quality As a final set of tie-breakers, prioritize schedules that improve the general student experience by optimizing these metrics in order of preference: Lower the avg_max (the average slot of a student's final exam). You are an expert university registrar choosing the best final-exam schedule.
Use these definitions (lower is better unless stated otherwise):
• conflicts: 2 exams at the same time (requires makeup)
• quints: 5 in a row (requires makeup)
• quads: 4 in a row (requires makeup)
• four in five slots: 4 within 5 (requires makeup)
• triple in 24h (no gaps): 3 within 24h, consecutive (requires makeup)
• triple in same day (no gaps): 3 in one day, consecutive (requires makeup)
• three in four slots: 3 within 4 slots
• evening/morning b2b: evening into next morning
• other b2b: any other back-to-back pair
• two in three slots: 2 within 3 slots
• avg_max: average slot of each student's last exam (lower is earlier finish)


Your task: Identify the SINGLE BEST schedule from the options below.

Policy guidance: *Objective:* To generate an optimal final examination schedule that minimizes student stress and administrative burden by optimizing a specific set of metrics according to a tiered priority system.  *Context:* There are three exam slots available per day. Solutions must have a conflicts value of 0 or 1.  *Tier 1:* Minimize Mandatory Reschedules The highest priority is to minimize the metrics associated with intense exam clusters that require a mandatory reschedule. This objective is achieved by minimizing the sum of the following two metrics: evening/morning b2b other b2b  *Tier 3:* Enhance Overall Schedule Quality As a final set of tie-breakers, prioritize schedules that improve the general student experience by optimizing these metrics in order of preference: Lower the avg_max (the average slot of a student's final exam). You are an expert university registrar choosing the best final-exam schedule.
Use these definitions (lower is better unless stated otherwise):
• conflicts: 2 exams at the same time (requires makeup)
• quints: 5 in a row (requires makeup)
• quads: 4 in a row (requires makeup)
• four in five slots: 4 within 5 (requires makeup)
• triple in 24h (no gaps): 3 within 24h, consecutive (requires makeup)
• triple in same day (no gaps): 3 in one day, consecutive (requires makeup)
• three in four slots: 3 within 4 slots
• evening/morning b2b: evening into next morning
• other b2b: any other back-to-back pair
• two in three slots: 2 within 3 slots
• avg_max: average slot of each student's last exam (lower is earlier finish)


Your task: Identify the SINGLE BEST schedule from the options below.

Policy guidance: *Objective:* To generate an optimal final examination schedule that minimizes student stress and administrative burden by optimizing a specific set of metrics according to a tiered priority system.  *Context:* There are three exam slots available per day. Solutions must have a conflicts value of 0 or 1.  *Tier 1:* Minimize Mandatory Reschedules The highest priority is to minimize the metrics associated with intense exam clusters that require a mandatory reschedule. Please minimize the following metrics in the order listed: quints (five exams in 24 hours) quads (four exams in 24 hours) four in five slots triple in 24h (no gaps) triple in same day (no gaps)  *Tier 2:* Reduce Student Exam Fatigue Once Tier 1 objectives have been met, the primary tie-breaker is to minimize the total number of back-to-back exams. This objective is achieved by minimizing the sum of the following two metrics: evening/morning b2b other b2b  *Tier 3:* Enhance Overall Schedule Quality As a final set of tie-breakers, prioritize schedules that improve the general student experience by optimizing these metrics in order of preference: Lower the avg_max (the average slot of a student's final exam). You are an expert university registrar choosing the best final-exam schedule.
Use these definitions (lower is better unless stated otherwise):
• conflicts: 2 exams at the same time (requires makeup)
• quints: 5 in a row (requires makeup)
• quads: 4 in a row (requires makeup)
• four in five slots: 4 within 5 (requires makeup)
• triple in 24h (no gaps): 3 within 24h, consecutive (requires makeup)
• triple in same day (no gaps): 3 in one day, consecutive (requires makeup)
• three in four slots: 3 within 4 slots
• evening/morning b2b: evening into next morning
• other b2b: any other back-to-back pair
• two in three slots: 2 within 3 slots
• avg_max: average slot of each student's last exam (lower is earlier finish)


Your task: Identify the SINGLE BEST schedule from the options below.

Policy guidance: *Objective:* To generate an optimal final examination schedule that minimizes student stress and administrative burden by optimizing a specific set of metrics according to a tiered priority system.  *Context:* There are three exam slots available per day. Solutions must have a conflicts value of 0 or 1.  *Tier 1:* Minimize Mandatory Reschedules The highest priority is to minimize the metrics associated with intense exam clusters that require a mandatory reschedule. Please minimize the following metrics in the order listed: quints (five exams in 24 hours) quads (four exams in 24 hours) four in five slots triple in 24h (no gaps) triple in same day (no gaps)  *Tier 2:* Reduce Student Exam Fatigue Once Tier 1 objectives have been met, the primary tie-breaker is to minimize the total number of back-to-back exams. This objective is achieved by minimizing the sum of the following two metrics: evening/morning b2b other b2b  *Tier 3:* Enhance Overall Schedule Quality As a final set of tie-breakers, prioritize schedules that improve the general student experience by optimizing these metrics in order of preference: Lower the avg_max (the average slot of a student's final exam).
